name: Miraflores de la Sierra, Puertos de Canencia y Rascafría, Embalse de Pinilla – circular route
name_original: Miraflores de la Sierra, Puertos de Canencia y Rascafría, Embalse de Pinilla – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Canencia are situated within the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range, offering varied terrain for cyclists. The region features gentle undulations and challenging climbs, particularly in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama. Cyclists can traverse dense Scots pine forests, oak groves, and birch trees, with routes often accompanied by streams. The area provides panoramic views of the Lozoya Valley and the Peñalara massif. Best road cycling routes around Canencia The most popular road cycling route is Miraflores de la Sierra, Puertos de Canencia y Rascafría, Embalse de Pinilla – circular route, a 39.9 miles (64.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 35 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied mountain terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Plaza de España, Rascafría – La Morcuera Pass loop from Bustarviejo, a difficult 41.2 miles (66.3 km) path. This route offers substantial climbing and views of the surrounding Sierra de Guadarrama. Local road cyclists also love the Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint – Navafría Pass loop from El Tomillar, a 23.3 miles (37.6 km) trail leading through mountain passes and forested areas, often completed in about 2 hours 5 minutes. Road cycling around Canencia is defined by mountain passes, dense forests, and panoramic views of the Lozoya Valley. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Canencia are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 40 reviews. More than 7000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Canencia's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Canencia?
A: Canencia offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 60 tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and difficult options.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes in Canencia?
A: The road cycling routes in Canencia are primarily moderate to difficult, with 27 moderate and 29 difficult routes. There are also 4 easy routes available for those seeking a less challenging ride. The terrain often includes significant elevation gain and mountain passes.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes around Canencia?
A: Cyclists in Canencia can expect diverse and scenic landscapes. Routes often traverse dense Scots pine forests, oak groves, and striking birch trees, especially beautiful in autumn. You'll also find gentle undulations, challenging climbs in the foothills of the Sierra de Guadarrama, and panoramic views of the Lozoya Valley and the majestic Peñalara massif.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or viewpoints along the routes?
A: Yes, the region is rich in natural beauty. You might encounter the serene Canencia Waterfall (Chorrera de Mojonavalle), particularly vibrant in spring. Viewpoints like the Mirador de la Chorrera offer splendid views of the Canencia Valley. Other notable points include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5299820" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1651353" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Morcuera Pass&lt;/a&gt;, which are often part of popular cycling routes.

Q: What cultural or historical attractions can be found near the road cycling routes in Canencia?
A: Beyond the natural allure, you can explore cultural and historical points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2545853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of San Blas&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful views of the surrounding landscape, including La Pedriza and the distant Madrid skyline. The historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2715157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Royal Monastery of Santa María de El Paular&lt;/a&gt;, nestled among forests in nearby Rascafría, is also a significant attraction.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Canencia?
A: Spring and autumn are generally ideal for road cycling in Canencia. Spring brings vibrant greenery and increased water flow to features like the Canencia Waterfall. Autumn transforms the birch trees into golden hues, offering particularly picturesque rides. Summers can be warm, while winters may present colder conditions and potential snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Canencia?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Canencia are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977039526/miraflores-de-la-sierra-puertos-de-canencia-y-rascafria-embalse-de-pinilla-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miraflores de la Sierra, Puertos de Canencia y Rascafría, Embalse de Pinilla – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31803324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de España, Rascafría – La Morcuera Pass loop from Bustarviejo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Canencia?
A: The road cycling experience in Canencia is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 40 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied mountain terrain, challenging climbs, and the stunning panoramic views of the Lozoya Valley and surrounding Sierra de Guadarrama.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families or less experienced cyclists?
A: While many routes are moderate to difficult, there are 4 easy road cycling routes available in Canencia. These offer a gentler introduction to the region's beauty, though it's always advisable to check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's or group's capabilities.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Canencia?
A: Parking is generally available in the villages and starting points of many routes around Canencia. It's recommended to check specific route descriptions or local information for designated parking areas, especially for popular trailheads or passes like Canencia Pass.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat near the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the villages within and around the Canencia region, such as Canencia itself, Rascafría, or Bustarviejo, offer cafes, restaurants, and pubs where cyclists can refuel and relax. These establishments are often located conveniently near popular starting or ending points of routes.

Q: Are there any specific routes that offer particularly scenic views of reservoirs?
A: Several routes provide excellent views of the region's reservoirs. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977039526/miraflores-de-la-sierra-puertos-de-canencia-y-rascafria-embalse-de-pinilla-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Miraflores de la Sierra, Puertos de Canencia y Rascafría, Embalse de Pinilla – circular route&lt;/a&gt; passes by the Pinilla Reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5148338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla Reservoir Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable highlight in the area, often incorporated into rides.
tips: The viewpoint has incredible views, not only of the reservoir but also of Guadarrama and Peñalara in the background. A quiet place, full of peace, and a good place to rest.

Rascafría is a beautiful village in the heart of the Sierra de Guadarrama mountain range. Located in the Lozoya Valley, it boasts an ideal location for cycling between the Morcuera and Cotos mountain passes.

Its main monument is the Monastery of Santa María del Paular, nestled in a privileged setting blanketed with pine and oak forests. As its name suggests, it is one of the coldest villages in the Madrid region.
A popular and well-visited spring with clean, sweet water.
